summary: Primary regression check PASSED — no infinite loading spinner after login. Signed in as testsignout_1783230265@example.com; app routed within ~4s to the new profile-error screen (not to /home). testID='profile-error-message' shows 'No profile found. The database schema may not be applied yet.' and testID='profile-error-signout' is a 162x48 tappable button. Tapping it correctly navigates to /login (login-email-input visible). Sign-out regression also verified through this same path. IMPORTANT SIDE-FINDING FOR MAIN AGENT: the review request describes testsignout_1783230265@example.com as a 'known-good' account that 'should route successfully to /home' — but in practice it hits the profile-error path. That means the Supabase user_profiles row for this account is missing (either the schema/trigger fix in /app/supabase_schema.sql was never executed in project jnkdhrgtulruwjlpfxhl, or the row got deleted). The bug fix (graceful error screen) is working exactly as designed, but no user account currently lands on /home, so the 'happy path' (profile loaded → role-based redirect to /(customer)/home) could not be validated end-to-end. Account tab / signout-button regression from iteration_2 could not be re-run because we never reached the customer tabs.

integration_issues.issue: testsignout_1783230265@example.com hits profile-error path — no user_profiles row exists for it in Supabase. Cannot validate happy-path redirect to /(customer)/home. Apply /app/supabase_schema.sql (trigger that creates user_profiles on auth.users insert) and/or manually INSERT a row for this uid, or sign up a fresh user AFTER the trigger is applied.

action_items: Apply the Supabase schema/trigger in /app/supabase_schema.sql to project jnkdhrgtulruwjlpfxhl so new signups auto-create a user_profiles row. Without this, every user will land on the error screen.
Once schema is applied, re-run happy-path test (fresh signup → expect redirect to /(customer)/home with signout-button visible on Account tab).
critical_code_review_comments: app/index.tsx correctly guards on !profile with an actionable error screen (proper testIDs, 48px touch target, uses theme tokens) — clean fix.
auth-context.tsx.loadProfile handles error, empty-data, and thrown-exception branches distinctly and always toggles profileError — good separation.
signOut in auth-context.tsx wraps supabase.auth.signOut() in try/catch and always clears local session/profile then router.replace('/(auth)/login') — safe even if network fails.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the fix under review (2/2 assertions: no-spinner + signout-from-error routes to /login)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The infinite-loading bug fix is verified. However the ONLY known account currently reaches the error screen — not /home. If retesting is required, first ensure /app/supabase_schema.sql has been applied in Supabase project jnkdhrgtulruwjlpfxhl, then sign up a fresh user and expect a redirect to /(customer)/home (with testID='signout-button' visible on the Account tab).
rca_of_issue: Root cause of previously reported 'App keeps loading...' was app/index.tsx only rendering ActivityIndicator whenever !profile, with no error state exposed by auth-context. Fix: auth-context now sets profileError on error/empty/throw in loadProfile, and index.tsx renders a dedicated error screen with a Sign-out CTA. Verified via Playwright at mobile viewport (375x667): after supabase.auth.signInWithPassword success, index.tsx routes to the error screen within ~4s (not an infinite spinner), and the profile-error-signout button navigates back to /login.
